data.title: SciDev cuts FY26 revenue guidance but flags stronger FY27 outlook
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SciDev Ltd ( (AU:SDV) ) just unveiled an update.

SciDev reported its third consecutive quarter of positive underlying EBITDA in 3Q FY26, with margin improving to 6% and Process Chemistry on track for record full-year revenue, underpinned by fully implemented cost reductions that lower corporate expenses and position the group for stronger earnings from FY27. However, the company cut FY26 revenue guidance to A$82–87 million from A$100–110 million, citing cyclical cost-pressure headwinds in Energy Services and weather and shipping-related delays at the Rum Jungle project that defer, rather than cancel, revenue into FY27, while management highlights a growing pipeline of customer trials and expects a meaningful earnings uplift next year.

SciDev’s Energy Services division has faced intensified competition in commoditised friction-reduction chemicals and slower uptake of higher-value specialty products as upstream E&P clients push harder on costs, driving around 80% of the guidance downgrade. In Water Technologies, heavy rain and cyclone activity in the Northern Territory, plus global shipping disruptions, have shifted a substantial portion of Rum Jungle rehabilitation revenue into FY27, even as the project remains on budget, while ongoing cost-cutting, a pivot to a channel-partner model internationally and new contract wins in key U.S. basins support management’s confidence in the company’s FY27 earnings trajectory.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SDV) stock is a Buy with a A$0.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SciDev Ltd stock, see the AU:SDV Stock Forecast page.

More about SciDev Ltd

SciDev Ltd is an Australian-based industrial chemistry and water technology company listed on the ASX, supplying process chemistry, energy services chemicals and water treatment solutions to resources, energy and infrastructure markets. Its key business units include Process Chemistry, Energy Services and Water Technologies, with a growing focus on recurring revenue contracts and major remediation projects such as the Rum Jungle mine rehabilitation.

Average Trading Volume: 203,741

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$34.22M

An announcement from Kiwi Property Group ( (KWIPF) ) is now available.

Kiwi Property Group has scheduled the release of its annual results for the financial year ended 31 March 2026 for Monday, 18 May 2026. The company will host a live webcast and conference call for analysts, investors and media at 10:00 a.m. NZT on the day, underlining its efforts to maintain transparent communication with capital markets stakeholders around its performance and portfolio strategy.

The timing of the announcement will be closely watched by investors given Kiwi Property’s scale in the listed New Zealand property sector and its focus on mixed-use, retail and office assets. The dedicated webcast and call provide an opportunity for the market to gain further insight into operating conditions in the commercial property sector and the company’s ongoing approach to managing its high-quality real estate portfolio.

More about Kiwi Property Group

Kiwi Property Group (NZX: KPG) is one of the largest listed property companies on the New Zealand Stock Exchange and a member of the S&P/NZX 20 Index. The company owns and manages a diversified portfolio of mixed-use, retail and office buildings across New Zealand, aiming to provide investors with reliable exposure to high-quality domestic real estate through active asset management.

data.title: Viva Energy Moves to Restore Geelong Refinery After Gasoline Unit Fire
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Viva Energy Group Ltd. ( (AU:VEA) ) has shared an update.

Viva Energy has reported a fire in the Alkylation Unit of the Gasoline complex at its Geelong Refinery, which occurred on the evening of 15 April and was fully extinguished the following morning without injuries. Key processing units such as the crude distillation units and reformer were unaffected, though the residue catalytic cracking unit is currently offline while operations are stabilised.

In the near term, the refinery expects to operate at about 80% capacity for diesel and jet fuel and 60% for petrol, but says existing fuel stocks should allow it to maintain normal supply to customers. The company aims to restart the residue catalytic cracking unit within weeks, lift output to more than 90% of capacity while repairs proceed, and has begun assessing damage and financial impact under insurance coverage, underscoring Geelong’s strategic importance and the company’s commitment to restoring full operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:VEA) stock is a Hold with a A$2.15 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Viva Energy Group Ltd. stock, see the AU:VEA Stock Forecast page.

More about Viva Energy Group Ltd.

Viva Energy Group Ltd. is an Australian energy and convenience retail company that operates a nationwide network of more than 1,280 retail convenience and fuel stores and supplies fuels and lubricants to nearly 1,550 service stations. The group owns and operates the Geelong Refinery in Victoria and runs bulk fuels, aviation, bitumen, marine, chemicals, polymers and lubricants businesses supported by extensive terminal and airport infrastructure across the country.

YTD Price Performance: 25.87%

Average Trading Volume: 8,784,133

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Astro Resources NL ( (AU:VMS) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Astro Resources NL has disclosed a change in the holdings of director Anthony Leibowitz, who has increased his stake in the company through a substantial on-market purchase. Leibowitz, via his associated entities including Kalonda Pty Ltd, acquired 645,000 fully paid ordinary shares on 17 April 2026 at a total value of $100,474.88, signalling strengthened alignment between the director and shareholders without any corresponding disposals.

The transaction adds to Leibowitz’s existing mix of performance rights, ordinary shares and listed options held directly and through related entities, demonstrating continued confidence in the company’s prospects. The additional investment may be viewed positively by the market as it enhances the director’s financial exposure to Astro Resources’ future performance and underscores long-term support for the company’s strategic direction.

data.title: China First Capital Group Sets 2026 AGM, Seeks Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China First Capital Group Ltd. ( (HK:1269) ) has issued an update.

China First Capital Group Limited has called its annual general meeting for 9 June 2026 in Shenzhen, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and related board and auditor reports. The agenda includes the re-election of executive and independent non-executive directors and authorisation for the board to set their remuneration for 2026.

Shareholders will also vote on reappointing Linksfield CPA Limited as auditor and granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding any treasury shares. The share issuance mandate, a common practice among Hong Kong-listed firms, would give the board additional flexibility to raise capital or pursue corporate actions, potentially affecting existing shareholders through future equity dilution.

More about China First Capital Group Ltd.

China First Capital Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands. While the notice does not detail its business lines, it operates as a listed entity on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong and is governed by the Listing Rules and its Articles of Association.

data.title: Pioneer Wins Key Permit to Reopen Idaho Mine Road and Accelerate Critical Minerals Exploration
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Pioneer Lithium Limited ( (AU:PMM) ) is now available.

Pioneer Minerals has secured a U.S. Forest Service Road Use Permit to reopen the historic Springfield Mine access road at its North Pine Project in Idaho, enabling clearing and maintenance along existing National Forest System roads. By leveraging historic road infrastructure, the company expects to cut capital costs, avoid helicopter-supported mobilisation and improve operational flexibility for exploration crews.

The restored access will accelerate field work including electromagnetic geophysical surveys, geological sampling and preparation for drilling at the Springfield Prospect, which has already returned high-grade tungsten, gallium and gold results indicating a multi-commodity critical mineral system. This milestone advances Springfield toward resource definition and potential low-impact development, strengthening Pioneer’s position in critical minerals and supporting its evaluation of modular processing options and potential U.S. funding avenues.

More about Pioneer Lithium Limited

Pioneer Minerals Limited is an Australian-listed exploration company focused on critical and precious minerals, with a key asset in the North Pine Project in Idaho, U.S. The company is targeting a multi-commodity system comprising tungsten, gallium, gold and silver, aiming to position itself within emerging domestic critical mineral supply chains and related U.S. government support programs.

Average Trading Volume: 71,298

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: NAB Lifts Provisions and Tweaks Capital Policy to Shore Up Balance Sheet
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

National Australia Bank Limited ( (AU:NAB) ) just unveiled an announcement.

National Australia Bank has moved to bolster its balance sheet in response to heightened market volatility stemming from the conflict in the Middle East, increasing forward-looking collective provisions and adjusting its dividend reinvestment plan. The bank expects first-half 2026 credit impairment charges of $706 million, driven by higher economic adjustments and sector overlays for industries exposed to fuel supply and cost risks.

These changes, alongside interest rate volatility, a weaker New Zealand dollar, and an overlay on internally rated credit models, have pressured NAB’s Common Equity Tier 1 ratio, prompting a 1.5% discounted and partially underwritten DRP expected to raise up to $1.8 billion and add about 40 basis points to capital in the second half. NAB has also revised its software capitalisation policy, shortening asset lives, tightening what can be capitalised, and lifting the capitalisation threshold to $20 million, which will increase expensed technology investment from the second half of 2026 and may dampen reported earnings while aligning with peer practice and technological change.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:NAB) stock is a Sell with a A$39.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on National Australia Bank Limited stock, see the AU:NAB Stock Forecast page.

More about National Australia Bank Limited

National Australia Bank Limited is a major Australian financial institution operating in retail, business, and institutional banking, as well as wealth management. It provides a broad range of banking and financial services to customers in Australia and New Zealand, with a focus on maintaining strong capital and risk management positions in a volatile macroeconomic and geopolitical environment.

Average Trading Volume: 4,858,962

The latest announcement is out from Yidu Tech, Inc. ( (HK:2158) ).

Yidu Tech Inc. expects to swing to a net profit of about RMB55 million to RMB70 million for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, compared with a net loss of roughly RMB135.2 million a year earlier. Profit attributable to owners is estimated at RMB41 million to RMB56 million, versus a loss of about RMB117.8 million in the prior fiscal year.

Management attributes this turnaround to a surge in new orders across its core segments, driven by continuous upgrades that embed AI into its products and strengthen their value proposition. Higher gross margins, supported by the greater value-add of AI-integrated offerings, operational efficiency gains, and economies of scale, are also expected to underpin the improved financial performance and signal stronger industry positioning for the group.

More about Yidu Tech, Inc.

Yidu Tech Inc. is a Hong Kong–listed technology company focused on AI-powered products and solutions, operating through several core business segments. The group targets data-driven, high-value applications where upgraded AI capabilities and operational efficiency can enhance competitiveness and profitability.

data.title: NEXTDC launches A$2.2bn plan after record 250MW utilisation surge
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Nextdc Limited ( (AU:NXT) ) is now available.

NEXTDC has reported a transformational jump in contracted utilisation, adding 250MW at its S4 Western Sydney facility to lift pro forma contracted capacity to 667MW and the forward order book to 544MW as of 31 March 2026. The surge, driven by strong hyperscale and AI demand, is expected to deliver contracted EBITDA of more than A$1.0bn from existing agreements, highlighting the company’s deepening revenue visibility and reinforcing its position in Australia’s data centre market.

To fund the accelerated rollout of S4 and a broader A$2.2bn capital plan, NEXTDC is launching a fully underwritten A$1.5bn equity entitlement offer and expanding its hybrid securities programme by A$700m, backed by a total A$1.7bn commitment from La Caisse. These moves will lift pro forma liquidity to about A$5.9bn and support roughly A$1.5bn of accelerated investment in S4 through FY27, while helping de‑risk Western Sydney developments ahead of potential private capital partnerships from 2027.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:NXT) stock is a Buy with a A$16.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nextdc Limited stock, see the AU:NXT Stock Forecast page.

More about Nextdc Limited

NEXTDC Limited is an Australian data centre operator that designs, builds and operates carrier- and cloud-neutral facilities serving hyperscale, enterprise and AI-focused customers. The company’s primary business is providing high‑capacity, high‑reliability colocation and infrastructure services to support rapidly growing cloud computing and artificial intelligence workloads, with a significant development focus in Western Sydney.

data.title: NEXTDC Seeks Trading Halt Ahead of Major Equity Raising
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Nextdc Limited ( (AU:NXT) ).

NEXTDC Limited has requested a trading halt on its ordinary shares as it prepares to announce the outcome of the institutional component of an accelerated entitlement offer. The suspension, granted under ASX Listing Rule 17.1, is tied to a proposed material equity raising via a pro-rata non-renounceable offer of new fully paid shares.

Trading in NEXTDC’s securities is expected to remain halted until the announcement is released or until the market opens on 22 April 2026, whichever comes first. The move signals a significant capital-raising initiative that could strengthen the company’s balance sheet and fund growth, with timing and structure aimed at swiftly securing institutional support.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:NXT) stock is a Buy with a A$16.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nextdc Limited stock, see the AU:NXT Stock Forecast page.

More about Nextdc Limited

NEXTDC Limited is an Australian data centre operator listed on the ASX, providing colocation and related digital infrastructure services. The company focuses on delivering scalable, carrier-neutral facilities that support enterprise and cloud service providers across key Australian markets.
